description: ParseBeef on BEEF_V2 data must return a non-nil transaction from which TxID() can be called without panicking. Go SDK prior to v1.2.21 returned nil for the transaction field when parsing BEEF_V2, causing a nil-pointer panic on TxID().

regression.symptom: go-sdk ParseBeef() returned nil transaction for BEEF_V2 format, causing nil-pointer dereference when caller invoked TxID()
regression.root_cause: BEEF_V2 parse path did not assign the newest transaction to the return value, unlike the ATOMIC_BEEF and BEEF_V1 paths

vectors.description: Minimal BEEF_V2 encoded as hex — parse must succeed and TxID must be callable
vectors.notes: 0200beef = BEEF_V2 magic (LE uint32 0xBEEF0002). 000000 = 0 BUMPs, 0 txs. Minimal valid BEEF_V2 envelope. No transactions means txid is null/nil — but parse must not panic.

vectors.description: v1 new BEEF round-trip — minimum BEEF_V1 bytes encode correctly
vectors.notes: 0100beef = BEEF_V1 magic (LE uint32 0xBEEF0001). 00 = 0 BUMPs, 00 = 0 txs. Minimum valid BEEF_V1. TS SDK returns null txid without error. Go SDK (as of v1.2.23) errors in NewBeefFromTransaction(nil) when ParseBeef's BEEF_V1 path produces a nil transaction. Marked intended: Go SDK parity fix needed.
